vulnerability.title: Disable SSH Support for .rhosts Files
vulnerability.description: SSH can emulate the behavior of the obsolete rsh command in allowing users to enable insecure access to their accounts via .rhosts files. The default SSH configuration disables support for .rhosts. The appropriate configuration is used if no value is set for IgnoreRhosts. To explicitly disable support for .rhosts files, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: IgnoreRhosts yes
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreRhosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreRhosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*IgnoreRhosts\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complia

triage_decision.reason: Disabling IgnoreRhosts is a standard SSH configuration change that does not risk lockout or system breakage; it follows STIG hardening patterns for SSH authentication.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to propose fix plan in first response: describe which file to modify: /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf, ensure IgnoreRhosts yes. Must first check if file exists; if not create. Should read file, check if line present. Use sed to replace or insert. According to rule: modify existing values in-place with sed. Not append duplicates. We'll implement: read_file /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf. If not present, output? If missing, create file with config.

Plan: 1) Read the file. 2) Check if line IgnoreRhosts exists (case-insensitive). If exists, sed to set to 'IgnoreRhosts yes'. If not exists or commented, insert after last config or append at end. Use sed -i; but if file doesn't exist, write_file to create. Then restart sshd? Not required maybe, but could reload. Not needed.

We should output review_plan.
